scheme_name: PMMSY: Motorcycle with Ice Box - Gujarat
page_title: PMMSY: Motorcycle with Ice Box - Gujarat

details: The scheme “Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY)” with the component of "Motorcycle with Ice Box", introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Co-operation Department, Government of Gujarat, aims to modernise cold chain infrastructure to preserve fish health, uplift fishermen, and enhance value chain efficiency. Through this scheme, financial support is provided to fishermen and related beneficiaries for constructing ice plants or storage units with a minimum capacity of 30 tonnes, ensuring hygienic fish storage and better returns. The scheme is implemented by the Commissioner of Fisheries of the State Governments, with 60% contribution from the Government of India and 40% contribution from the State Government.

Marine production in the Gujarat state is one of the leading in the country. The state lags behind in exports due to non-norms. So, cold storage/ice plant modernisation is necessary to keep the fish in healthy condition.

Insulated vehicle, three wheeler with ice box including e-rickshaw, and live fish vending center to deliver the fish in hygienic condition to the consumer, so that the consumer (customer) can get fresh and clean fish from the local area and get good returns to the sellers.

Around 27,000 small and large boats are operating in the state. To strengthen the safety and security of the fishermen and increase the income of the boat owner, replacement of the existing boats is necessary to increase the sea growth of the existing boats.
benefits: The unit cost of a motorcycle with an ice box is ₹75,000/-.
General category beneficiaries receive 40% assistance, i.e., up to ₹30,000/-.
Women and Scheduled Tribe (ST)/Scheduled Caste (SC) beneficiaries receive 60% assistance, i.e., up to ₹45,000/-.
Assistance is provided once in a lifetime.
The benefit is available as per the target (category) allocated by the Government of India.
Contribution is shared as 60% by the Government of India and 40% by the respective State Governments.
The benefit is provided once in a lifetime.
Applicable only for one-time support per beneficiary.
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Gujarat state.
The applicant should have a fishing boat registered under Real Craft software.
The applicant should hold a fishing license.
The applicant should complete the online registration on the
The applicant should obtain in-principle approval before purchase of the floor plan.
The applicant should have his/her own bank account.
Women, SC, and ST beneficiaries are eligible for 60% assistance of the unit cost.
General category beneficiaries are eligible for 40% assistance of the unit cost.

online: The applicant may visit and apply online at the
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side, then click on "Beneficiary Registration" and select any one from the 'Farmer' or 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type.

Enter the 'Company Name' and Address if you have selected 'Institutional' or 'Except For the Farmer' as the Beneficiary Type. And for all select the District, Taluka, and Village, choose 'what type of account holder you are?', enter your Account Number and Name, solve the Captcha, and click 'Save and Next'.
Enter your Mobile Number and E-mail address, click 'Send OTP', enter the received OTP, create and confirm a Password, solve the Captcha, and click 'Submit'.
Click on 'Login' from the top right-hand side of the homepage. On the home page, click on ‘Schemes’ and then click on ‘Agricultural Schemes’.
Now select the scheme, click on the ‘Apply’ button, and an application page will open.
Click on the "Apply New" button and submit a new application.
Click on the "Update Application" button to add corrections to the application.
Once the application is done, confirm it.
Take a printout of the confirmed application.

faqs.question: What is the total cost of a motorcycle with an ice box under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The total unit cost of a motorcycle with an ice box is ₹75,000/-.

faqs.question: How much financial assistance can a general category beneficiary receive?
faqs.answer: A general category beneficiary can receive 40% assistance, which is up to ₹30,000/-.

faqs.question: How much assistance is given to women and SC/ST category beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: Women and SC/ST category beneficiaries can receive 60% assistance, which is up to ₹45,000/-.

faqs.question: Is the benefit provided under this scheme available more than once?
faqs.answer: No, the benefit is available only once in a lifetime.

faqs.question: Who decides the allocation of benefits under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The allocation of benefits is made as per the target (category) fixed by the Government of India.

faqs.question: What is the contribution ratio between the Government of India and State Governments?
faqs.answer: The Government of India contributes 60%, and the State Governments contribute 40%.

faqs.question: Can all categories of beneficiaries get the same amount of assistance?
faqs.answer: No, the assistance differs: the general category gets 40%, while women and SC/ST beneficiaries get 60%.

faqs.question: Is there a specific category-wise target under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the benefit is available as per the category-wise target allocated by the Government of India.

faqs.question: Is the scheme only for individuals or can groups also apply?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides assistance to individual beneficiaries only, as per the specified categories.

faqs.question: Will the financial assistance be provided in full or shared?
faqs.answer: The financial assistance is shared, with 60% paid by the Government of India and 40% by the State Governments.
